item.title: One-Pager Brief on Pangram Labs
item.description: pspanPangram Labs builds the most accurate AI text detector in the world. Team is &gt;25 FTE; they are active on Twitter, you can engage directly, look for "affiliates" tab of @pangram./span/ppspanHere is a table of their performance on adversarially modified AI text (/spana href="https://arxiv.org/

item.title: Extinction risk is not the right first sentence
item.description: h1spanMoney wins quiet fights but votes win louder ones. What actually moves the public to act on AI, and why it decides which regulations and safeguards survive./span/h1pispanThis is my first post here. My leaning into AI safety is still in it's formative phase but I come at it as a tech founder an

item.title: Independent alignment of language models
item.description: blockquotepspanThe user could write up the metaethical argument — the one developed in Part One, refined — and submit it as feedback to Anthropic, publish it, or engage with researchers working on AI alignment and values. item.title: Introduction for and Reactions to Plan A
item.description: h4Introducing Plan A/h4pThe folks who brought you a href="https://ai-2027.com/"AI 2027/a, a so far remarkably accurate set of predictions despite those predictions having seemed freaky to many at the time, now bring you their positive vision that involves more freaky predictions: a href="https://ai-

item.title: so far at least, i'm pretty sure AI has been net job-creating. this was not what i expected--although i was much less pessimistic than others, i thought by this level of capability we'd have seen some
item.description: pso far at least, i'm pretty sure AI has been net job-creating.br /br /this was not what i expected--although i was much less pessimistic than others, i thought by this level of capability we'd have seen some impact.br /br /it is possible this direction keeps going!/p

item.title: Theories of Deep Learning
item.description: pspanThis field has been blessed with exponential empirical success in the form of architectures and algorithms that simply worked through scaling, while the theory lagged behind/spanspan class="footnote-reference" id="fnrefmtk3tjutja"supa href="https://www.lesswrong.com/feed.xml#fnmtk3tjutja"[1]/a/

item.description: pAI 2040 and other similar policy proposals for dystopian AI control are evil. br /br /If I had the time machine in Terminator, I would send every one of these people back in time to live in Stalin's Russia, or Mao's China, or Berlin with the Wall and then bring them back after a year cured of thei

item.title: Great comment from a subscriber who commented in our Chat on his direct experience. Agentic AI is pretty difficult to implement at scale in production because (1) a bunch of new types of systems need
item.description: pGreat comment from a subscriber who commented in our Chat on his direct experience. br /br /Agentic AI is pretty difficult to implement at scale in production because (1) a bunch of new types of systems need to be built that have never existed before (orchestration /routing, context engineering, st

item.title: Measuring Is Not Enough Anymore
item.description: pspanIs measuring general AI capabilities a good strategy to reduce AI existential risk, compared to other strategies? A disclaimer upfront: To answer that, one organization in particular will serve as an example, but the argument that we'll be making applies just as much or little across all of AI

item.title: Don’t bring an AI detector to a deepfake fight: proving reality through multimodal provenance
item.description: pspanEpistemic status: confident on the framing, speculative on the implementation/span/pblockquotepbspanTL;DR:/span/bspan Fake media detectors are on the losing end of an arms race. Instead of trying to spot fakes, we need to distrust images and videos by default, unless we can prove they're real.

item.title: A Simple Model of AI "Psychosis"
item.description: pspan"AI Psychosis" has gone from an evocative term /spanspanfor people undergoing extreme delusions, sometimes even culminating in suicide/spanspan, to the now colloquial insult for anyone who's just a little /spanispantoo/span/ispan into LLMs./span/ppspanWhen it's not just being overused, I think
